meta.note: GitHub Issues were not available without authentication — cross-referencing could not be performed. The CVE check requires access to external databases. Container and infrastructure security findings are kept separately in .agents/analysis/docker_analysis.json (SEC-Dxx) and are not duplicated here.

DEBUG.issue: The default fallback in production.py is the string 'True'. If the DEBUG key is missing from the secrets file, DEBUG will be True in production.

SECRET_KEY.source: get_secret('SECRET_KEY') from Docker secrets or sample_secrets_db.json
SECRET_KEY.issue: No hardcoded SECRET_KEY in the production code. The sample file contains the trivial value 'secret', but it is an intentional placeholder.

ALLOWED_HOSTS.status: OK — correctly configured via secrets in production

SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER.status: OK — the application is behind an NGINX proxy

USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST.status: OK — expected when running behind a proxy

missing_secure_headers.SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS: not set — HSTS not ensured at the Django level
missing_secure_headers.note: The NGINX proxy may provide these headers at the network layer — must be verified in proxy/default.conf. Nevertheless, the Django Security checklist recommendation is to set them at the Django level as well.

SESSION_ENGINE.note: Session data stored in a cookie — signed (HMAC), but not encrypted. The session size is limited to about 4 KB. Sensitive data must not be stored in the session.

CLAMD.note: ClamAV antivirus file scanning is active only in production — in dev it is deactivated (intentional).

secrets_management.mechanism: Docker secrets + fallback to sample JSON files

db_secrets.sample_content_risk: Contains trivial values (SECRET_KEY='secret', DB_PASS='test_secretpass') — an intentional placeholder for the dev environment

mail_secrets.issue: The Mailtrap credentials in the commit look like real sandbox credentials. If these values are active, they are accessible to anyone with access to the repository. Mailtrap sandbox credentials allow reading captured emails.

redis_secrets.mechanism: direct reading of the file via get_plain_redis_pass() / get_redis_pass()

inactive_user_behavior.current: Inactive users: raises ValidationError instead of returning False
inactive_user_behavior.correct_pattern: user_can_authenticate() should return False for inactive users (the Django standard)
inactive_user_behavior.risk: The calling code may unexpectedly catch the exception; or fail to catch it and cause a 500 error instead of a standard login rejection

cas_integration.note: CAS SSO is mentioned in AGENTS.md for the uzivatel application, but no direct CAS implementation was found in the audited files — probably in middleware or in unaudited parts of uzivatel/.

rest_framework.issue: BasicAuthentication transmits the credentials with every request (Base64, not encryption). Safe only over HTTPS. DRF without SessionAuthentication — CSRF protection is not necessary.
rest_framework.severity: Low — acceptable behind an HTTPS proxy

recaptcha.production: RECAPTCHA keys from the secrets file

known_vulnerabilities.note: The CVE check requires access to external databases (pip audit, safety check, OSV) — it could not be performed offline.
known_vulnerabilities.recommendation: Run 'pip audit' or 'safety check -r webclient/requirements.txt' in the CI pipeline for continuous CVE monitoring.

known_vulnerabilities.note: Additional security findings at the Docker image and infrastructure level (open ports, image versions, secret injection) are described in .agents/analysis/docker_analysis.json under the identifiers SEC-D01…SEC-D06 and are considered part of the overall security footprint of the system.

known_vulnerabilities.usage: webclient/uzivatel/models.py:28 — import of cached_property from distlib.util
known_vulnerabilities.note: distlib is an internal pip dependency, not intended for direct use in application code. See BUG-003.

csrf_xss_issues.pattern: mark_safe("'%s'" % str(getattr(mc, message, 'Message constant not found')))
csrf_xss_issues.description: The result of mark_safe depends on the values in core.message_constants. The constants are developer-defined — the direct XSS risk is low. However, the pattern is fragile: if a constant contained an apostrophe or HTML, it could be injected into the JS context.

The `get_ident_cely_link` property returns an HTML link with the ident_cely value from the database. If ident_cely is not escaped in the property, stored XSS is possible. The property implementation on the relevant model must be audited.
csrf_xss_issues.action_needed: Verify the implementation of the `get_ident_cely_link` property on all models where it is defined

csrf_xss_issues.description: The `new_instance` variable comes from attribute access to Dokument.extra_data. If the value contains unescaped user input, XSS is possible.
csrf_xss_issues.action_needed: Verify what the accessed Dokument.extra_data attribute returns and whether the value is escaped

csrf_xss_issues.description: The field name from the instance is returned as mark_safe. It depends on the implementation of `get_name()` in the specific field class.
csrf_xss_issues.action_needed: Verify the implementation of `get_name()` in all field classes in vypis/fields.py

csrf_xss_issues.description: All uses are hardcoded HTML without user input — safe. These are widget extensions for crispy-forms.
Most uses wrap hardcoded constants (numbers, 'true', 'logoutFunction'). Exception: `mark_safe(_(translation_key))` — the translated texts are under the developers' control.

raw_sql_usage.query: select id, ident_cely from public.archeologicky_zaznam where ident_cely like %s
GIS queries (ST_MakeEnvelope, ST_Within, ST_AsGML, etc.) + Pian.objects.raw()
raw_sql_usage.user_input: bbox coordinates from the HTTP request — but always parameterized via %s
raw_sql_usage.risk: Low — parameterized queries protect against SQL injection

raw_sql_usage.user_input: The filter value from a form
raw_sql_usage.risk: Low — parameterized. Note: .extra() deprecated in Django 4.0.
None — a Celery task without user input

raw_sql_usage.query: Model.objects.raw(query_select) — four times for Pian, SamostatnyNalez, Projekt, DokumentExtraData
raw_sql_usage.risk: None — a management command, SELECT without a WHERE from user input

raw_sql_usage.query: f-string SQL for terminating DB connections (pg_terminate_backend)
raw_sql_usage.risk: Low — test code, the value comes from connection.settings_dict (internal configuration)
